scope.principle: Pericope-first mapping. Direct depiction outranks thematic resemblance; stillness outranks substitution.
scope.not_exhaustive_objects: Comprehensive at the major Gospel-pericope level for this research pass; not an enumeration of every duplicate impression in each museum.

relation_definitions.DIRECT: The institutional title, description, or subject record identifies the same Gospel episode.
relation_definitions.COMPOSITE: The episode appears within a multi-scene or multi-pericope composition.
relation_definitions.RELATED: The work is iconographically adjacent but is not an exact depiction.
relation_definitions.GAP: No sufficiently direct candidate retained in this pass.

status_definitions.existing_pin: Already present in RISE and previously contact-sheet reviewed.
status_definitions.needs_contact_sheet: Rights/identity look viable, but human visual review is still required.
status_definitions.needs_athena_id: Cleveland accession discovered; resolve numeric Athena ID or expand the adapter.
status_definitions.needs_persistent_id: Resolve the Rijks Linked Art persistent numeric ID before pinning.
status_definitions.hold: Do not put into an exact pericope pool without explicit composite/related treatment.

atlas_amendments.change: The current RijksData Search documentation exposes title, description, aboutActor, creator and other axes; English and Dutch search terms are supported, with Dutch coverage more complete. creationDate also supports wildcard patterns such as 16??. The Museum Atlas should no longer state that exact-year creationDate is the only useful route.

The official API accepts either an Athena ID or accession number for a specific object, but RISE's current adapter rejects dotted accession numbers with /^\d+$/. New discoveries listed by accession therefore need numeric ID resolution or a deliberate adapter amendment.
